    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 79
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/79](https://www.erdosproblems.com/79)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíWe say $G$ is *Ramsey size linear* if $R(G,H) \ll m$ for all graphs $H$ with $m$ edges
    25‚Üíand no isolated vertices.
    26‚Üí
    27‚ÜíAre there infinitely many graphs $G$ which are not Ramsey size linear but such
    28‚Üíthat all of their proper subgraphs are?
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[EFRS93] ErdŇĎs, P., Faudree, R., Rousseau, C., and Schelp, R., 1993.
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üí[Wi24] Wigderson, A., 2024.
    33‚Üí-/

    35‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üínamespace Erdos79
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- `IsSubgraphOf H G` means $H$ is isomorphic to a subgraph of $G$: there exists an
    40‚Üí    injection from $V(H)$ to $V(G)$ that preserves adjacency. -/
    41‚Üídef IsSubgraphOf {őĪ ő≤ : Type*} (H : SimpleGraph őĪ) (G : SimpleGraph ő≤) : Prop :=
    42‚Üí  ‚ąÉ f : őĪ ‚Üí ő≤, Function.Injective f ‚ąß ‚ąÄ u v, H.Adj u v ‚Üí G.Adj (f u) (f v)
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí/-- The Ramsey property: every 2-coloring of $K_n$ contains $G$ in one color or $H$ in the
    45‚Üí    other. A 2-coloring of $K_n$ is a graph $S$ on `Fin n`; one color class is $S$ and the
    46‚Üí    other is $S^c$ (the complement). -/
    47‚Üídef RamseyProp {p q : ‚Ąē} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin p)) (H : SimpleGraph (Fin q))
    48‚Üí    (n : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    49‚Üí  ‚ąÄ S : SimpleGraph (Fin n), IsSubgraphOf G S ‚ą® IsSubgraphOf H SŠ∂ú
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí/-- A graph has no isolated vertices if every vertex has at least one neighbor. -/
    52‚Üídef NoIsolatedVertices {q : ‚Ąē} (H : SimpleGraph (Fin q)) : Prop :=
    53‚Üí  ‚ąÄ v : Fin q, ‚ąÉ w : Fin q, H.Adj v w
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí/-- A graph $G$ is Ramsey size linear if there exists $C > 0$ such that for every graph $H$
    56‚Üí    with no isolated vertices, the Ramsey property $R(G,H)$ holds at some
    57‚Üí    $n \leq C \cdot |E(H)|$. -/
    58‚Üídef IsRamseySizeLinear {p : ‚Ąē} (G : SimpleGraph (Fin p)) : Prop :=
    59‚Üí  ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÄ (q : ‚Ąē) (H : SimpleGraph (Fin q)) [DecidableRel H.Adj],
    60‚Üí    NoIsolatedVertices H ‚Üí
    61‚Üí    ‚ąÉ n : ‚Ąē, (n : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ C * (H.edgeFinset.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚ąß RamseyProp G H n
    62‚Üí
    63‚Üí/-- **ErdŇĎs Problem 79**: Are there infinitely many graphs which are not Ramsey
    64‚Üí    size linear but all of whose proper subgraphs are? A graph $H$ is a proper subgraph
    65‚Üí    of $G$ if $H$ embeds into $G$ (as a subgraph) but $G$ does not embed into $H$.
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí    Asked by ErdŇĎs, Faudree, Rousseau, and Schelp [EFRS93].
    68‚Üí    Proved by Wigderson [Wi24]. $K_4$ is the only explicitly known example. -/
    69‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    70‚Üítheorem erdos_79 : answer(True) ‚ÜĒ
    71‚Üí    ‚ąÄ N : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÉ (p : ‚Ąē) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin p)),
    72‚Üí      p ‚Č• N ‚ąß
    73‚Üí      ¬¨ IsRamseySizeLinear G ‚ąß
    74‚Üí      ‚ąÄ (q : ‚Ąē) (H : SimpleGraph (Fin q)),
    75‚Üí        IsSubgraphOf H G ‚Üí
    76‚Üí        ¬¨ IsSubgraphOf G H ‚Üí
    77‚Üí        IsRamseySizeLinear H := by
    78‚Üí  sorry
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üíend Erdos79

1‚Üí# AI Review: ErdŇĎs Problem 79
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üí## 1. Code Reuse
     4‚Üí
     5‚Üí**Major issue.** Problem 79 defines four concepts from scratch in a local `Erdos79` namespace that already exist (or have superior equivalents) in `FormalConjecturesForMathlib` and Mathlib:
     6‚Üí
     7‚Üí| Local definition (79.lean) | Library equivalent | Location |
     8‚Üí|---|---|---|
     9‚Üí| `Erdos79.IsSubgraphOf H G` | `SimpleGraph.IsContained H G` (notation `H ‚äĎ G`) | Mathlib `SimpleGraph.Copy` |
    10‚Üí| `Erdos79.RamseyProp G H n` | (implicit via `SimpleGraph.sizeRamsey`) | `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/.../SizeRamsey.lean` |
    11‚Üí| `Erdos79.NoIsolatedVertices H` | `‚ąÄ v, 0 < H.degree v` | Standard Mathlib idiom |
    12‚Üí| `Erdos79.IsRamseySizeLinear G` | `SimpleGraph.IsRamseySizeLinear G` | `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/.../SizeRamsey.lean` |
    13‚Üí
    14‚ÜíProblem 567 (a closely related problem about specific Ramsey-size-linear graphs) already uses the library's `SimpleGraph.IsRamseySizeLinear` directly via `ProblemImports`. Problem 79 should do the same.
    15‚Üí
    16‚ÜíThe local `IsSubgraphOf` is a manual version of Mathlib's `SimpleGraph.Copy`/`IsContained` infrastructure. Using `IsContained` would align with the rest of the codebase (problems 59, 60, 566, 567 all use it) and avoid redundant definitions.
    17‚Üí
    18‚Üí**Recommendation:** Delete all four local definitions and rewrite the theorem using `SimpleGraph.IsRamseySizeLinear` and `SimpleGraph.IsContained` from the library.
    19‚Üí
    20‚Üí## 2. Citations
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíThe website [erdosproblems.com/79](https://www.erdosproblems.com/79) lists three references:
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí| Citation | On website | In formalization |
    25‚Üí|---|---|---|
    26‚Üí| [EFRS93] ErdŇĎs, Faudree, Rousseau, Schelp (1993) | Yes | Yes (but incomplete ‚ÄĒ no title or journal) |
    27‚Üí| [Er95] ErdŇĎs (1995) | Yes | **Missing** |
    28‚Üí| [Wi24] Wigderson (2024) | Yes | Yes (but incomplete ‚ÄĒ no title) |
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí**Issues:**
    31‚Üí- **[Er95] is entirely missing.** The website lists ErdŇĎs (1995) as a reference.
    32‚Üí- **[EFRS93] lacks full bibliographic details.** Compare with problem 567 which cites it as: `ErdŇĎs, Faudree, Rousseau and Schelp, _Ramsey size linear graphs_. Combin. Probab. Comput. (1993), 389-399.` Problem 79 only gives `ErdŇĎs, P., Faudree, R., Rousseau, C., and Schelp, R., 1993.`
    33‚Üí- **[Wi24] lacks full bibliographic details.** Only `Wigderson, A., 2024.` is given with no title or venue.
    34‚Üí
    35‚Üí**Recommendation:** Expand all citations to include title and journal/venue information, matching the style used in problem 567. Add the missing [Er95] reference.
    36‚Üí
    37‚Üí## 3. Variants
    38‚Üí
    39‚ÜíThe problem as stated on the website asks:
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí> Are there infinitely many graphs G which are not Ramsey size linear but such that all of their proper subgraphs are?
    42‚Üí
    43‚ÜíThe formalization captures exactly this single question. No additional variants are listed on the website. The formalization is complete in this regard.
    44‚Üí
    45‚ÜíHowever, the docstring mentions that "$K_4$ is the only explicitly known example" ‚ÄĒ this could potentially be formalized as a separate statement (e.g., asserting that $K_4$ is not Ramsey size linear but all its proper subgraphs are), which would serve as a concrete sanity check and would be more directly verifiable. This is optional but would add value.
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí## 4. Readability
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí**Several readability concerns:**
    50‚Üí
    51‚Üí1. **Redundant definitions hurt readability.** Having local definitions for `IsSubgraphOf`, `RamseyProp`, `NoIsolatedVertices`, and `IsRamseySizeLinear` when library versions exist forces the reader to mentally verify equivalence. Using library definitions would make the statement immediately recognizable.
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üí2. **The `RamseyProp` definition is non-standard.** The local `RamseyProp` uses a 2-coloring model where one color class is `S` and the other is `SŠ∂ú` (complement). This differs from the library's model which uses `R ‚Č§ F` and `F \ R`. The complement-based version is actually modeling a *partition* of the complete graph's edges, which is correct for Ramsey theory on vertex colorings of $K_n$, but the library's formulation (coloring edges of an arbitrary graph $F$) is more standard for *size* Ramsey theory.
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí3. **The `NoIsolatedVertices` definition** uses `‚ąÉ w, H.Adj v w` instead of the simpler and more standard `0 < H.degree v`. The latter is a single comparison; the former requires the reader to unfold the existential.
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí4. **Proper subgraph definition is inline.** The notion of "proper subgraph" (embeds into $G$ but $G$ doesn't embed into $H$) is expressed inline in the theorem statement rather than being given a named definition. This is fine for a single use but slightly impacts readability.
    58‚Üí
    59‚Üí## 5. Formalizability
    60‚Üí
    61‚Üí**Assessment: Highly formalizable, with one subtle point.**
    62‚Üí
    63‚ÜíThe problem statement is precise and well-defined. The key concepts ‚ÄĒ Ramsey size linearity, proper subgraphs, and "infinitely many" ‚ÄĒ all have clear formal meanings.
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí**Subtle point on "infinitely many graphs":** The formalization quantifies as `‚ąÄ N : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÉ (p : ‚Ąē) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin p)), p ‚Č• N ‚ąß ...`, meaning it asks for graphs on arbitrarily many vertices. This is a reasonable encoding of "infinitely many," since there are only finitely many graphs on any fixed vertex set. However, it technically allows the same graph (up to isomorphism) to appear on different vertex sets via padding with isolated vertices. This is a very minor issue and arguably doesn't affect the mathematical content, since any non-Ramsey-size-linear graph can't be trivially padded without changing its Ramsey properties.
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí**Ambiguity level: Low.** The statement is precise enough that the formalization is essentially forced.
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí## 6. Correctness
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí**The formalization is mathematically correct but has a significant structural flaw:**
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí### Correct aspects:
    74‚Üí- The notion of "Ramsey size linear" is correctly captured: ‚ąÉ C > 0 such that for all H with no isolated vertices, the Ramsey number is ‚Č§ C ¬∑ |E(H)|.
    75‚Üí- "Proper subgraph" as `IsSubgraphOf H G ‚ąß ¬¨ IsSubgraphOf G H` correctly captures "H embeds into G but G does not embed into H."
    76‚Üí- The answer is correctly tagged as `True` and the problem is marked as `solved`, consistent with Wigderson's 2024 result.
    77‚Üí
    78‚Üí### Structural flaw ‚ÄĒ conceptual mismatch between `RamseyProp` and `IsRamseySizeLinear`:
    79‚Üí
    80‚ÜíThe local `IsRamseySizeLinear` is defined using `RamseyProp`, which concerns the *Ramsey number* $R(G,H)$ (vertex Ramsey on complete graphs). However, the standard definition of "Ramsey size linear" concerns the *size Ramsey number* $\hat{r}(G,H)$ (edge count of a minimal graph whose 2-colorings force a copy). These are **different quantities**.
    81‚Üí
    82‚ÜíThe local definition says: there exists $n ‚Č§ C ¬∑ |E(H)|$ such that every 2-coloring of $K_n$ contains $G$ or $H^c$. This bounds the classical Ramsey number $R(G,H)$ linearly in $|E(H)|$.
    83‚Üí
    84‚ÜíThe standard definition (and the library's version) says: the minimum number of *edges* in a graph $F$ such that every 2-coloring of $F$'s edges contains $G$ or $H$ is at most $C ¬∑ |E(H)|$. This bounds $\hat{r}(G,H)$ linearly in $|E(H)|$.
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí**These are not equivalent.** The size Ramsey number $\hat{r}(G,H)$ can be much smaller than $\binom{R(G,H)}{2}$ (the edge count of $K_{R(G,H)}$). The library's `SimpleGraph.IsRamseySizeLinear` uses the correct *size* Ramsey number formulation via `sizeRamsey`. The local definition in problem 79 uses the classical *vertex* Ramsey number formulation.
    87‚Üí
    88‚ÜíThe problem name "Ramsey size linear" specifically refers to the *size* Ramsey number being linear, so the library definition is correct and the local definition is wrong.
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üí**This is the most critical issue in the formalization.**
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí### Summary
    93‚Üí
    94‚Üí| Aspect | Assessment |
    95‚Üí|---|---|
    96‚Üí| Code reuse | Poor ‚ÄĒ four redundant local definitions; library versions exist and are used elsewhere |
    97‚Üí| Citations | Incomplete ‚ÄĒ [Er95] missing; [EFRS93] and [Wi24] lack titles/venues |
    98‚Üí| Variants | Complete ‚ÄĒ single variant correctly captured |
    99‚Üí| Readability | Fair ‚ÄĒ redundant definitions and non-standard idioms hurt clarity |
   100‚Üí| Formalizability | High ‚ÄĒ low ambiguity, statement is precise |
   101‚Üí| Correctness | **Incorrect** ‚ÄĒ uses classical Ramsey number instead of size Ramsey number; should use `SimpleGraph.IsRamseySizeLinear` from the library |
   102‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 569
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíIs it true that for every $k \geq 1$, there exists a constant $c_k$ such that for any graph $H$
    23‚Üíwith $m$ edges and no isolated vertices, $R(C_{2k+1}, H) \leq c_k \cdot m$?
    24‚Üí
    25‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/569](https://www.erdosproblems.com/569)
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üí[EFRS93] ErdŇĎs, P., Faudree, R., Rousseau, C., and Schelp, R.
    28‚Üí-/
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    31‚Üí
    32‚Üínamespace Erdos569
    33‚Üí
    34‚Üí/-- A graph $G$ embeds into a graph $H$: there is an injective map from
    35‚Üívertices of $G$ to vertices of $H$ preserving adjacency. -/
    36‚Üídef Embeds {V W : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (H : SimpleGraph W) : Prop :=
    37‚Üí  ‚ąÉ f : V ‚Üí W, Function.Injective f ‚ąß ‚ąÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚Üí H.Adj (f u) (f v)
    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- The Ramsey number $R(G, H)$: the minimum $N$ such that for any graph $F$
    40‚Üíon $\operatorname{Fin} N$, either $G$ embeds in $F$ or $H$ embeds in the complement
    41‚Üíof $F$. -/
    42‚Üínoncomputable def ramseyNum {V W : Type*}
    43‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) (H : SimpleGraph W) : ‚Ąē :=
    44‚Üí  sInf {N : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÄ (F : SimpleGraph (Fin N)),
    45‚Üí    Embeds G F ‚ą® Embeds H FŠ∂ú}
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí/-- The cycle graph $C_m$ on $m$ vertices ($m \geq 3$). Vertex $i$ is adjacent to vertex
    48‚Üí$i + 1 \pmod{m}$ and vertex $i - 1 \pmod{m}$. -/
    49‚Üídef cycleGraph (m : ‚Ąē) (_ : m ‚Č• 3) : SimpleGraph (Fin m) where
    50‚Üí  Adj i j := i ‚Č† j ‚ąß (j.val = (i.val + 1) % m ‚ą® i.val = (j.val + 1) % m)
    51‚Üí  symm := fun _ _ ‚ü®hne, h‚ü© => ‚ü®hne.symm, h.elim Or.inr Or.inl‚ü©
    52‚Üí  loopless := fun _ ‚ü®h, _‚ü© => h rfl
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/--
    55‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 569 [EFRS93]:
    56‚Üí
    57‚ÜíFor every $k \geq 1$, there exists a constant $c_k$ such that for any graph $H$
    58‚Üíwith $m$ edges and no isolated vertices, $R(C_{2k+1}, H) \leq c_k \cdot m$.
    59‚Üí
    60‚ÜíHere $C_{2k+1}$ is the odd cycle on $2k+1$ vertices.
    61‚Üí-/
    62‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    63‚Üítheorem erdos_569 (k : ‚Ąē) (hk : k ‚Č• 1) :
    64‚Üí    ‚ąÉ c : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ (n : ‚Ąē) (H : SimpleGraph (Fin n)),
    65‚Üí      (‚ąÄ v : Fin n, ‚ąÉ w, H.Adj v w) ‚Üí

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 78
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/78](https://www.erdosproblems.com/78)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíGive a constructive proof that $R(k) > C^k$ for some constant $C > 1$, where $R(k)$ is the
    25‚Üídiagonal Ramsey number. ErdŇĎs's probabilistic proof that $R(k) \gg k \cdot 2^{k/2}$ is
    26‚Üínon-constructive. Prize: \$100.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíPartial progress: Cohen [Co15] constructed a graph on $n$ vertices with no clique or independent
    29‚Üíset of size $\geq 2^{(\log \log n)^C}$. Li [Li23b] improved this to $(\log n)^C$.
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíSee also OEIS sequence A059442 and related problems #77, #1029.
    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí[Er69b] ErdŇĎs, P., _Some applications of Ramsey's theorem to additive number theory_.
    34‚ÜíEuropean J. Combin. (1969).
    35‚Üí
    36‚Üí[Er71] ErdŇĎs, P., _On some extremal problems on r-graphs_. Discrete Math. **1** (1971), 1‚Äď6.
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí[Er88] ErdŇĎs, P., _Problems and results on chromatic numbers in finite and infinite graphs_.
    39‚ÜíGraph Theory with Applications to Algorithms and Computer Science (1988).
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí[Er93] ErdŇĎs, P., _On some of my favourite theorems_. Combinatorics, Paul ErdŇĎs is Eighty,
    42‚ÜíVol. 2 (1993), p. 337.
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí[Er95] ErdŇĎs, P., _Some of my favourite problems in various branches of combinatorics_.
    45‚ÜíCongressus Numerantium 107 (1995).
    46‚Üí
    47‚Üí[Er97c] ErdŇĎs, P., _Some recent problems and results in graph theory_. Discrete Math.
    48‚Üí**164** (1997), 81‚Äď85.
    49‚Üí
    50‚Üí[Va99] Vaughan, R. C., _On the number of monochromatic complete subgraphs_. (1999), 3.49.
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí[Co15] Cohen, D., _Two-source dispersers for polylogarithmic entropy and improved Ramsey
    53‚Üígraphs_. (2015).
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üí[Li23b] Li, Z., _Explicit Ramsey graphs and two-source extractors_. (2023).
    56‚Üí-/
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üínamespace Erdos78
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí/--
    63‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 78 (Open, \$100 prize):
    64‚Üí
    65‚ÜíLet $R(k)$ be the Ramsey number for $K_k$, the minimal $n$ such that every
    66‚Üí2-colouring of the edges of $K_n$ contains a monochromatic copy of $K_k$.
    67‚ÜíGive a constructive proof that $R(k) > C^k$ for some constant $C > 1$.
    68‚Üí
    69‚ÜíErdŇĎs gave a simple probabilistic (non-constructive) proof that
    70‚Üí$R(k) \gg k \cdot 2^{k/2}$. This problem asks for an explicit/constructive
    71‚Üílower bound that is still exponential in $k$.
    72‚Üí
    73‚ÜíEquivalently, this asks for an explicit construction of a graph on $n$
    74‚Üívertices which does not contain any clique or independent set of size
    75‚Üí$\geq c \cdot \log(n)$ for some constant $c > 0$.
    76‚Üí
    77‚ÜíWe formalize the core mathematical content: there exists $C > 1$ such that
    78‚Üífor all $k \geq 2$, there exists a graph on at least $C^k$ vertices with no
    79‚Üíclique or independent set of size $k$ (an independent set of size $k$ in $G$
    80‚Üíis equivalent to a clique of size $k$ in the complement $G^c$, by
    81‚Üí`SimpleGraph.isClique_compl`). The "constructive" requirement pertains
    82‚Üíto the nature of the proof, not the formal statement itself.
    83‚Üí-/
    84‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
    85‚Üítheorem erdos_78 :
    86‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C : ‚ĄĚ, C > 1 ‚ąß ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, k ‚Č• 2 ‚Üí
    87‚Üí      ‚ąÉ n : ‚Ąē, (C ^ k : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§ ‚ÜĎn ‚ąß
    88‚Üí        ‚ąÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
    89‚Üí          G.CliqueFree k ‚ąß GŠ∂ú.CliqueFree k := by
    90‚Üí  sorry
    91‚Üí
    92‚Üí/--
    93‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 78 ‚ÄĒ $o(\sqrt{n})$ variant [Er69b]:
    94‚Üí
    95‚ÜíErdŇĎs also asked for an explicit construction of a graph on $n$ vertices
    96‚Üíwith no clique or independent set of size $o(\sqrt{n})$. This is weaker
    97‚Üíthan the full exponential lower bound. The website notes this is now known.
    98‚Üí-/
    99‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
   100‚Üítheorem erdos_78_variant_sqrt :
   101‚Üí    ‚ąÉ (f : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē), (‚ąÄ n, f n < n) ‚ąß
   102‚Üí      Filter.Tendsto (fun n => (f n : ‚ĄĚ) / n ^ (1/2 : ‚ĄĚ)) Filter.atTop (nhds 0) ‚ąß
   103‚Üí      ‚ąÄ n, ‚ąÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n), G.CliqueFree (f n) ‚ąß GŠ∂ú.CliqueFree (f n) := by
   104‚Üí  sorry
   105‚Üí
   106‚Üí/--
   107‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 78 ‚ÄĒ logarithmic formulation:
   108‚Üí
   109‚ÜíEquivalently, there exists a constant $c > 0$ such that for all $n \geq 2$,
   110‚Üíthere is a graph on $n$ vertices with no clique or independent set of size
   111‚Üí$\geq c \cdot \log(n)$. This is logically equivalent to `erdos_78` but
   112‚Üíphrased in the "construct a graph on $n$ vertices" framing.
   113‚Üí-/
   114‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
   115‚Üítheorem erdos_78_log :
   116‚Üí    ‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, c > 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• 2 ‚Üí
   117‚Üí      ‚ąÉ G : SimpleGraph (Fin n),
   118‚Üí        G.CliqueFree ‚Ćąc * Real.log n‚ĆČ‚āä ‚ąß GŠ∂ú.CliqueFree ‚Ćąc * Real.log n‚ĆČ‚āä := by
   119‚Üí  sorry
   120‚Üí
   121‚Üíend Erdos78

    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 567
    21‚Üí
    22‚ÜíLet $G$ be either $Q_3$ or $K_{3,3}$ or $H_5$ (the last formed by adding two vertex-disjoint chords
    23‚Üíto $C_5$). Is it true that, if $H$ has $m$ edges and no isolated vertices, then
    24‚Üí$$ \hat{r}(G,H) \ll m? $$
    25‚Üí
    26‚ÜíIn other words, is $G$ Ramsey size linear? A special case of Problem 566.
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/567](https://www.erdosproblems.com/567)
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[EFRS93] ErdŇĎs, Faudree, Rousseau and Schelp, _Ramsey size linear graphs_.

    36‚Üíopen SimpleGraph

    38‚Üí
    39‚Üí/-- $Q_3$ is the 3-dimensional hypercube graph (8 vertices, 12 edges).
    40‚ÜíVertices are 3-bit vectors. Two vertices are adjacent iff they differ in exactly one bit. -/
    41‚Üídef Q3 : SimpleGraph (Fin 3 ‚Üí Bool) where
    42‚Üí  Adj u v := #{i | u i ‚Č† v i} = 1
    43‚Üí  symm _ _ := by simp [eq_comm]
    44‚Üí  loopless _ := by simp
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí/-- $K_{3,3}$ is the complete bipartite graph with partition sizes 3, 3 (6 vertices, 9 edges). -/
    47‚Üídef K33 : SimpleGraph (Fin 3 ‚äē Fin 3) := completeBipartiteGraph (Fin 3) (Fin 3)
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí/-- $H_5$ is $C_5$ with two vertex-disjoint chords (5 vertices, 7 edges).
    50‚ÜíAlso known as $K_4^*$ (the graph obtained from $K_4$ by subdividing one edge). -/
    51‚Üídef H5 : SimpleGraph (Fin 5) :=
    52‚Üí  .cycleGraph 5 ‚äĒ .edge 0 2 ‚äĒ .edge 1 3
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí/--
    55‚Üí**ErdŇĎs Problem 567 (Q3)**

    57‚ÜíIs $Q_3$ (the 3-dimensional hypercube) Ramsey size linear?

    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 560
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/560](https://www.erdosproblems.com/560)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíLet $\hat{R}(G)$ denote the size Ramsey number, the minimal number of edges $m$ such
    25‚Üíthat there is a graph $H$ with $m$ edges such that in any 2-colouring of the
    26‚Üíedges of $H$ there is a monochromatic copy of $G$.
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíDetermine $\hat{R}(K_{n,n})$, where $K_{n,n}$ is the complete bipartite graph with $n$
    29‚Üívertices in each component.
    30‚Üí
    31‚ÜíKnown bounds:
    32‚Üí$$\frac{1}{60} n^2 \cdot 2^n < \hat{R}(K_{n,n}) < \frac{3}{2} n^3 \cdot 2^n$$
    33‚Üí
    34‚ÜíThe lower bound (for $n \geq 6$) was proved by ErdŇĎs and Rousseau [ErRo93].
    35‚ÜíThe upper bound was proved by ErdŇĎs, Faudree, Rousseau, and Schelp [EFRS78b]
    36‚Üíand NeŇ°etŇôil and R√∂dl [NeRo78].
    37‚Üí
    38‚ÜíConlon, Fox, and Wigderson [CFW23] conjecture that $\hat{R}(K_{n,n}) \asymp n^3 \cdot 2^n$.
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí[ErRo93] ErdŇĎs, P. and Rousseau, C.C., *The size Ramsey number of a complete bipartite
    41‚Üígraph*, Discrete Mathematics, 1993.
    42‚Üí
    43‚Üí[EFRS78b] ErdŇĎs, P., Faudree, R.J., Rousseau, C.C., and Schelp, R.H., *The size Ramsey
    44‚Üínumber*, Periodica Mathematica Hungarica, 1978.
    45‚Üí
    46‚Üí[NeRo78] NeŇ°etŇôil, J. and R√∂dl, V., *The Ramsey property for graphs with forbidden complete
    47‚Üísubgraphs*, Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series B, 1978.
    48‚Üí
    49‚Üí[CFW23] Conlon, D., Fox, J., and Wigderson, Y., *Ramsey numbers of books and
    50‚Üíquasirandomness*, Combinatorica, 2023.
    51‚Üí-/
    52‚Üí
    53‚Üíopen SimpleGraph
    54‚Üí
    55‚Üínamespace Erdos560
    56‚Üí
    57‚Üí/-- The size Ramsey number $\hat{R}(G)$: the minimum number of edges in a graph $H$
    58‚Üíthat is Ramsey for $G$.
    59‚Üí
    60‚ÜíA graph $H$ on $N$ vertices is Ramsey for $G$ if every 2-coloring of the edges
    61‚Üíof $H$ (represented as a symmetric function $c : \operatorname{Fin} N \to \operatorname{Fin} N \to \operatorname{Bool}$)
    62‚Üícontains a monochromatic copy of $G$, i.e., an injective map $f$ from the
    63‚Üívertices of $G$ into $\operatorname{Fin} N$ that preserves adjacency in $H$ and maps all
    64‚Üíedges to the same color. -/
    65‚Üínoncomputable def sizeRamseyNumber {V : Type*} [Fintype V]
    66‚Üí    (G : SimpleGraph V) : ‚Ąē :=
    67‚Üí  sInf {m : ‚Ąē | ‚ąÉ (N : ‚Ąē) (H : SimpleGraph (Fin N)),
    68‚Üí    Nat.card H.edgeSet = m ‚ąß
    69‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (c : Fin N ‚Üí Fin N ‚Üí Bool),
    70‚Üí      (‚ąÄ i j, c i j = c j i) ‚Üí
    71‚Üí      ‚ąÉ (b : Bool) (f : V ‚Üí Fin N),
    72‚Üí        Function.Injective f ‚ąß
    73‚Üí        (‚ąÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚Üí H.Adj (f u) (f v)) ‚ąß
    74‚Üí        (‚ąÄ u v, G.Adj u v ‚Üí c (f u) (f v) = b)}
    75‚Üí
    76‚Üí/--
    77‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 560, lower bound [ErRo93]:
    78‚Üí
    79‚ÜíFor all $n \geq 6$, $\hat{R}(K_{n,n}) > \frac{1}{60} n^2 \cdot 2^n$.
    80‚Üí-/
    81‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    82‚Üítheorem erdos_560.variants.lower_bound :
    83‚Üí    ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• 6 ‚Üí
    84‚Üí      (sizeRamseyNumber (completeBipartiteGraph (Fin n) (Fin n)) : ‚ĄĚ) >
    85‚Üí        (1 / 60 : ‚ĄĚ) * (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 2 * 2 ^ n := by
    86‚Üí  sorry
    87‚Üí
    88‚Üí/--
    89‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 560, upper bound [EFRS78b, NeRo78]:
    90‚Üí
    91‚ÜíFor all $n \geq 1$, $\hat{R}(K_{n,n}) < \frac{3}{2} n^3 \cdot 2^n$.
    92‚Üí-/
    93‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    94‚Üítheorem erdos_560.variants.upper_bound :
    95‚Üí    ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• 1 ‚Üí
    96‚Üí      (sizeRamseyNumber (completeBipartiteGraph (Fin n) (Fin n)) : ‚ĄĚ) <
    97‚Üí        (3 / 2 : ‚ĄĚ) * (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 3 * 2 ^ n := by
    98‚Üí  sorry
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí/--
   101‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 560, conjecture [CFW23]:
   102‚Üí
   103‚Üí$\hat{R}(K_{n,n}) \asymp n^3 \cdot 2^n$, i.e., there exist constants $C_1, C_2 > 0$ and $N_0$
   104‚Üísuch that for all $n \geq N_0$,
   105‚Üí$$C_1 \cdot n^3 \cdot 2^n \leq \hat{R}(K_{n,n}) \leq C_2 \cdot n^3 \cdot 2^n.$$
   106‚Üí-/
   107‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 5]
   108‚Üítheorem erdos_560 :
   109‚Üí    ‚ąÉ C‚āĀ : ‚ĄĚ, C‚āĀ > 0 ‚ąß ‚ąÉ C‚āā : ‚ĄĚ, C‚āā > 0 ‚ąß
   110‚Üí    ‚ąÉ N‚āÄ : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ n : ‚Ąē, n ‚Č• N‚āÄ ‚Üí
   111‚Üí      C‚āĀ * (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 3 * 2 ^ n ‚Č§
   112‚Üí        (sizeRamseyNumber (completeBipartiteGraph (Fin n) (Fin n)) : ‚ĄĚ) ‚ąß
   113‚Üí      (sizeRamseyNumber (completeBipartiteGraph (Fin n) (Fin n)) : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č§
   114‚Üí        C‚āā * (n : ‚ĄĚ) ^ 3 * 2 ^ n := by
   115‚Üí  sorry
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üíend Erdos560
   118‚Üí

1‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 87 ‚ÄĒ Review
     2‚Üí
     3‚Üí## 1. Code Reuse
     4‚Üí
     5‚Üí**`ContainsSubgraph` is duplicated across many files.** The definition at line 38:
     6‚Üí
     7‚Üí```lean
     8‚Üídef ContainsSubgraph {V U : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V) (H : SimpleGraph U) : Prop :=
     9‚Üí  ‚ąÉ f : U ‚Üí V, Function.Injective f ‚ąß ‚ąÄ u v : U, H.Adj u v ‚Üí G.Adj (f u) (f v)
    10‚Üí```
    11‚Üí
    12‚Üíis semantically equivalent to Mathlib's `SimpleGraph.IsContained` (notation `‚äĎ`) from `Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Copy`:
    13‚Üí
    14‚Üí```lean
    15‚Üístructure Copy (A : SimpleGraph őĪ) (B : SimpleGraph ő≤) where
    16‚Üí  toHom : A ‚Üíg B
    17‚Üí  injective' : Injective toHom
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üíabbrev IsContained (A : SimpleGraph őĪ) (B : SimpleGraph ő≤) := Nonempty (Copy A B)
    20‚Üí```
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí`ContainsSubgraph G H` is equivalent to `H ‚äĎ G` (note the argument reversal). The same duplication exists in Problem 62 (`62.lean:45`) and likely in many of the ~30 other files that reference `ContainsSubgraph`. Recommend replacing with `IsContained` from Mathlib, or at minimum factoring into a shared definition.
    23‚Üí
    24‚Üí**`graphRamseyNumber` and `diagRamsey` are widely reused but defined locally.** Grep shows these definitions appear across ~30 ErdŇĎs problem files. They should be consolidated into `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/Combinatorics/Ramsey.lean`, which already defines `hypergraphRamsey`. The diagonal graph Ramsey number `diagRamsey k` is a specialization of `graphRamseyNumber` for complete graphs and could be related to the existing `hypergraphRamsey 2 k` (the 2-uniform hypergraph Ramsey number), though the definitions use different formulations (edge-coloring of complete graphs vs. graph complement containment).
    25‚Üí
    26‚Üí## 2. Citations
    27‚Üí
    28‚ÜíCompared against [erdosproblems.com/87](https://www.erdosproblems.com/87):
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí| Aspect | Formalization | Website | Match? |
    31‚Üí|---|---|---|---|
    32‚Üí| Status | `research open` | Open | ‚úď |
    33‚Üí| AMS classification | `AMS 5` | Graph theory / Ramsey theory | ‚úď |
    34‚Üí| [Er95] citation | Present (docstring line 24) | Present | ‚úď |
    35‚Üí| [FaMc93] Faudree‚ÄďMcKay | Not cited | Cited: showed R(pentagonal wheel W) = 17 | ‚úó |
    36‚Üí| Yuval Wigderson observation | Not mentioned | Noted: R(G) ‚Čę 2^(k/2) via random coloring | ‚úó |
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí**Recommendation:** Add the [FaMc93] reference explicitly, since the docstring already alludes to the k=4 counterexample ("ErdŇĎs originally conjectured R(G) ‚Č• R(k), which fails already for k = 4") but does not credit Faudree and McKay. The full citation from the website is:
    39‚Üí
    40‚Üí> [FaMc93] Faudree, R. J. and McKay, B. D., *A conjecture of ErdŇĎs and the Ramsey number r(W)*, J. Combin. Math. Combin. Comput. **13** (1993), 23‚Äď31.
    41‚Üí
    42‚ÜíWigderson's observation (that R(G) ‚Čę 2^(k/2) for any G with Ōá(G) = k) is useful context showing the weak form is asymptotically consistent with best-known lower bounds for R(k), but is not strictly required in the docstring.
    43‚Üí
    44‚Üí## 3. Variants
    45‚Üí
    46‚ÜíThe formalization captures both variants mentioned on the website:
    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí| Variant | Formalized? | Location |
    49‚Üí|---|---|---|
    50‚Üí| **Weak form:** R(G) > (1‚ąíőĶ)^k R(k) for large k | ‚úď | `erdos_87` (line 61) |
    51‚Üí| **Strong form:** R(G) > c¬∑R(k) for large k | ‚úď | `erdos_87.variants.strong` (line 77) |
    52‚Üí| **Original (disproven):** R(G) ‚Č• R(k) | Mentioned in docstring, not formalized | ‚ÄĒ |
    53‚Üí
    54‚ÜíThe original conjecture R(G) ‚Č• R(k) is appropriately not formalized as a separate theorem since it is known to be false (Faudree‚ÄďMcKay, k = 4). The docstring at line 29 documents this. No missing variants.
    55‚Üí
    56‚ÜíThe website notes the problem "is trivial for őĶ ‚Č• 3/4 since R(k) ‚Č§ 4^k." This is not mentioned in the docstring but is a useful mathematical remark rather than a separate variant.
    57‚Üí
    58‚Üí## 4. Readability
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí**Strengths:**
    61‚Üí- Clear module-level docstring explaining the problem, its history, and both variants.
    62‚Üí- Good separation of helper definitions (`ContainsSubgraph`, `graphRamseyNumber`, `diagRamsey`) with their own docstrings.
    63‚Üí- Docstrings for both theorem statements include the precise mathematical inequality.
    64‚Üí
    65‚Üí**Minor suggestions:**
    66‚Üí- The docstring for `graphRamseyNumber` (line 41‚Äď44) says "the minimum N such that every simple graph G on N vertices either contains a copy of H as a subgraph or whose complement contains a copy of H." The phrase "or whose complement" is grammatically awkward; consider "or its complement contains a copy of H."
    67‚Üí- The definitions are clean and self-contained. If `IsContained` from Mathlib is adopted, the `‚äĎ` notation would improve readability of `graphRamseyNumber`.
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí## 5. Formalizability
    70‚Üí
    71‚ÜíThe problem is **precisely formalizable with no ambiguity**. Both the weak and strong forms are unambiguous inequalities relating the graph Ramsey number R(G) to the diagonal Ramsey number R(k), parameterized by the chromatic number Ōá(G) = k. The quantifier structure (‚ąÄőĶ, ‚ąÉK, ‚ąÄk‚Č•K, ‚ąÄG with Ōá(G)=k) is standard and maps directly to Lean.
    72‚Üí
    73‚ÜíThe only mild design choice is the restriction `őĶ < 1` in the weak form (line 62). This is mathematically harmless: for őĶ ‚Č• 1, we have (1‚ąíőĶ)^k ‚Č§ 0 for large k (alternating sign or zero), so the inequality is trivially satisfied since Ramsey numbers are positive. However, adding `őĶ < 1` avoids potential confusion with negative/oscillating factors and makes the mathematical intent clearer. This is a reasonable choice.
    74‚Üí
    75‚ÜíThe `answer(sorry)` pattern correctly reflects the open status of the problem.
    76‚Üí
    77‚Üí## 6. Correctness
    78‚Üí
    79‚Üí### Definition correctness
    80‚Üí
    81‚Üí**`ContainsSubgraph` (line 38):** Correctly captures "H is isomorphic to a subgraph of G" via an injective adjacency-preserving map. This is *not-necessarily-induced* subgraph containment (adjacency is preserved in one direction only: `H.Adj u v ‚Üí G.Adj (f u) (f v)`), which is the standard meaning in the Ramsey theory context. ‚úď
    82‚Üí
    83‚Üí**`graphRamseyNumber` (line 45‚Äď47):** Defined as `sInf {N | ‚ąÄ G : SimpleGraph (Fin N), ContainsSubgraph G H ‚ą® ContainsSubgraph GŠ∂ú H}`. This correctly formalizes the graph Ramsey number R(H): the minimum N such that every 2-coloring of K_N contains a monochromatic copy of H. The equivalence between "every graph on N vertices or its complement contains H" and "every 2-coloring of K_N has a monochromatic H" is standard. ‚úď
    84‚Üí
    85‚Üí**Edge case:** If the infimum set is empty (which cannot happen for finite H by Ramsey's theorem, but Lean's `sInf` on ‚Ąē returns 0 for empty sets), the value would be 0. This is not an issue in practice since the theorem only applies to finite graphs via `[Fintype V]`, but a `Nonempty` hypothesis on the set or a `[Fintype U]` constraint on `graphRamseyNumber` would make the definition more robust. This is a minor concern.
    86‚Üí
    87‚Üí**`diagRamsey` (line 50‚Äď51):** `graphRamseyNumber (‚ä§ : SimpleGraph (Fin k))` correctly gives R(K_k), the diagonal Ramsey number R(k, k). ‚úď
    88‚Üí
    89‚Üí### Theorem correctness
    90‚Üí
    91‚Üí**Weak form (`erdos_87`, line 61‚Äď67):**
    92‚Üí
    93‚Üí```lean
    94‚Üí‚ąÄ őĶ : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < őĶ ‚Üí őĶ < 1 ‚Üí
    95‚Üí‚ąÉ K : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, K ‚Č§ k ‚Üí
    96‚Üí‚ąÄ {V : Type*} [Fintype V] (G : SimpleGraph V),
    97‚Üí  G.chromaticNumber = k ‚Üí
    98‚Üí  (diagRamsey k : ‚ĄĚ) * (1 - őĶ) ^ k < (graphRamseyNumber G : ‚ĄĚ)
    99‚Üí```
   100‚Üí
   101‚ÜíThis reads: "For every 0 < őĶ < 1, there exists K such that for all k ‚Č• K, for every finite graph G with Ōá(G) = k, we have (1‚ąíőĶ)^k ¬∑ R(k) < R(G)." This faithfully captures the website statement "R(G) > (1‚ąíőĶ)^k R(k) for every graph G with Ōá(G) = k." ‚úď
   102‚Üí
   103‚ÜíThe `chromaticNumber` in Mathlib has type `‚Ąē‚ąě`. The equality `G.chromaticNumber = k` uses a coercion `k : ‚Ąē ‚Üí ‚Ąē‚ąě`, which correctly ensures the chromatic number is the finite value k. Since `[Fintype V]` is assumed, the chromatic number is always finite, so this coercion is appropriate. ‚úď
   104‚Üí
   105‚Üí**Strong form (`erdos_87.variants.strong`, line 77‚Äď83):**
   106‚Üí
   107‚Üí```lean
   108‚Üí‚ąÉ c : ‚ĄĚ, 0 < c ‚ąß
   109‚Üí‚ąÉ K : ‚Ąē, ‚ąÄ k : ‚Ąē, K ‚Č§ k ‚Üí
   110‚Üí‚ąÄ {V : Type*} [Fintype V] (G : SimpleGraph V),
   111‚Üí  G.chromaticNumber = k ‚Üí
   112‚Üí  c * (diagRamsey k : ‚ĄĚ) < (graphRamseyNumber G : ‚ĄĚ)
   113‚Üí```
   114‚Üí
   115‚ÜíThis reads: "There exists c > 0 and K such that for all k ‚Č• K, for every finite graph G with Ōá(G) = k, we have c ¬∑ R(k) < R(G)." This matches the website statement. ‚úď
   116‚Üí
   117‚Üí**Relationship between forms:** The strong form implies the weak form (take őĶ small enough that c > (1‚ąíőĶ)^k for large k, which holds since (1‚ąíőĶ)^k ‚Üí 0). The formalization correctly separates them as independent open problems. ‚úď
   118‚Üí
   119‚Üí**No mathematical flaws identified.** The formalization is correct and complete.
   120‚Üí
   121‚Üí### Summary
   122‚Üí
   123‚Üí| Category | Assessment |

   130‚Üí| Correctness | **Correct.** No mathematical flaws. Minor robustness concern with `sInf ‚ąÖ = 0` edge case. |
